nodes.description: ESOL solubility via mat-gram (SMI-TED) on this host's MAX serve
SMI-TED ESOL property prediction via mat-gram POST /embeddings (wraps local MAX)
instruction: Send JSON body with smiles set to a SMILES string or list of SMILES (e.g. "CCO" or ["CCO", "c1ccccc1"]).
Prefer when the user asks to predict solubility, ESOL, logS, embeddings, or numeric property values for SMILES.
Service runs task=esol (property mode) against MAX on this host.
Response includes mode, task, model path, embeddings, and predictions (log10 mol/L).

nodes.description: ZINC lead-like molecule database via a single SQL twin over zinc_leadlike on this host's Postgres
Natural-language SQL over zinc_leadlike via the single twin query_zinc_leadlike (MW/logP, ZINC ID, InChIKey, tranche, SMILES)
instruction: Forward the user message verbatim under message (keep any twin/query prefix).
Prefer when the user asks about ZINC molecules, lead-like screening, MW/logP ranges, ZINC IDs, InChIKeys, tranches, or SMILES search in the database.
Do not use this for solubility prediction — that is solubility_embeddings.
Current twin: query_zinc_leadlike. Required params (null unused filters): zinc_id, inchikey, tranche_name, smiles_pattern, mwt_min, mwt_max, logp_min, logp_max, net_charge, limit.
Service selects a SQL twin via explicit name or EmbeddingGemma + LanceDB, extracts params with Lisette, and queries Postgres on this host.
JSON response includes twin_name, row_count, rows, and optional excel (base64) when include_excel is true.
If the user explicitly wants a downloadable spreadsheet, set response_format=excel_file instead of json.

nodes.description: Describe the single ZINC SQL twin query_zinc_leadlike and its required parameters (no DB execution)
instruction: Forward the user message verbatim under message.
Prefer when the user asks what ZINC query params are required or what the twin can filter.
Returns the twin name, description, and required parameters.
